div1.entryFree:
Brass, an alloy of copper and zinc: pewter and b. Shr. II, 358. b. cannon, H5 III, 1, 11. b., cur! offerest me b.? IV, 4, 19. trumpet, send thy b. voice . . ., Troil. I, 3, 257. a leaf of b. Tit. IV, 1, 102.
Serving for tablets to write on, and hence a symbol of imperishable memory: with characters of b. Meas. V, 11. since nor b. nor stone nor parchment bears not one (example), Wint. I, 2, 360. live in b. H5 IV, 3, 97. H8 IV, 2, 45. hold up high in b. Troil. I, 3, 64. Emblem of hardness and strength: and b. eternal slave to mortal rage, Sonn. 64, 4. 65, 1. 107, 14. as if this flesh were b. impregnable, R2 III, 2, 168. walls of beaten b. Caes. I, 3, 93. bind them in b. Per. III, 1, 3. -- Denoting insensibility and obduracy: unless my nerves were b. or hammered steel, Sonn. 120, 4. can any face of b. hold longer out, LLL V, 2, 395.
